reasoning: The Lean statement correctly captures all elements of the informal claim. `[MetricSpace X]` gives the metric space structure; `[ConnectedSpace X]` encodes connectedness; `∃ x y : X, x ≠ y` precisely expresses "more than one point" (at least two distinct elements); and `¬ Countable (univ : Set X)` — via the `CoeSort (Set α) (Type u)` coercion — asserts that the subtype `↥univ` is not countable, which by `countable_univ_iff` in `Mathlib/Data/Set/Countable.lean` is equivalent to `Uncountable X`, i.e., X is uncountable. There is no drift in hypotheses, conclusion, quantifier structure, or types. Mathlib's `ConnectedSpace` class already requires nonemptiness, but this is redundant given `hX`, not a spurious strengthening.
